orgs.desc: Italian criminal organization of mafia type originating from Calabria. Recognized as a unitary criminal organization with a collegial leadership by the Italian Supreme Court in 2016. It is the only mafia present on all five continents, with an estimated global turnover of 150 billion euros as of 2022. Its main activity is drug trafficking, followed by involvement in public contracts, vote buying, extortion, usury, arms trafficking, gambling, and toxic waste disposal.

The Hells Angels Motorcycle Club is an international outlaw biker gang that expanded to Australia in 1975, establishing chapters in Melbourne and Sydney. It has approximately 250 members and 14 chapters in Australia, involved in drug trafficking, violent crimes, and money laundering through legitimate businesses. The club is legally classified as a criminal organization in Queensland and has been involved in conflicts with rival gangs such as the Comancheros and Bandidos.

The Camorra is an Italian Mafia-type criminal organization originating in Campania, dating to the 18th century. It is structured into individual clans, each led by a boss, and engages primarily in drug trafficking, racketeering, counterfeiting, and money laundering. The organization has a strong presence in other European countries, especially Spain, and maintains close ties with South American drug cartels. As of 2023, powerful groups include the Mazzarella clan and the Secondigliano Alliance.

The American Mafia is a highly organized Italian-American criminal society that emerged in the late 19th century in impoverished Italian immigrant neighborhoods in New York, Chicago, New Orleans, and other US cities. It is structured into families and engages in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, extortion, drug trafficking, and murder. The organization cooperates with Italian organized crime groups such as the Sicilian Mafia, Camorra, and 'Ndrangheta. It remains active today with over 3,000 members and associates.

Mexican transnational criminal organization dedicated to drug trafficking and other illicit activities. Founded in 1989 by Joaquín 'El Chapo' Guzmán, Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada, and others. Based in Culiacán, Sinaloa, it operates across much of Mexico and the US border, and is considered one of the most influential criminal organizations globally. Currently divided into factions led by Iván Archivaldo Guzmán Salazar (Los Chapitos) and Ismael Zambada Sicairos (La Mayiza).

Al-Qaeda is a pan-Islamist militant organization founded in 1988 by Osama bin Laden and other veterans of the Soviet-Afghan War. It aims to unite the Muslim world under a caliphate and has conducted major attacks including the 1998 U.S. embassy bombings and the September 11 attacks. Designated a terrorist group by the UN and many countries, it operates globally with affiliates in various regions. Its current leader is Saif al-Adel.

Transnational Salafi jihadist militant organization and unrecognized quasi-state that proclaimed a worldwide caliphate in 2014. Originating from al-Qaeda in Iraq, it controlled large territories in Iraq and Syria from 2013 to 2019, enforcing extremist Islamic law. Known for brutal violence, including genocides, beheadings, and terrorist attacks globally. After losing its Middle Eastern territories, it reverted to insurgency and maintains significant presence in Africa.

The Gambino family is one of the Five Families of New York and one of the most powerful in the United States. Originating from the Black Hand gang led by Ignazio Lupo and Giuseppe Morello in East Harlem, it evolved under Salvatore D'Aquila and later Al Mineo. The family was formally established as one of the Five Families by Salvatore Maranzano after the Castellammarese War. It remains active in organized crime activities such as gambling and racketeering.

Hezbollah is a Lebanese Shia Islamist political party and paramilitary organization founded in 1982 by Lebanese clerics in response to the Israeli invasion of Lebanon. It operates a social services network, runs Al-Manar TV, and holds seats in the Lebanese Parliament. Its paramilitary wing is considered equivalent to a medium-sized army. The group is part of the Axis of Resistance and has been banned by the Lebanese government since March 2026.

The Outlaws Motorcycle Club, also known as the American Outlaws Association, is an international motorcycle gang founded in 1935 in McCook, Illinois. Classified as an organized crime syndicate by law enforcement, it is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, money laundering, and violent acts. The club has over 5,000 members across 441 chapters in 43 countries and is still active.

The Black Mafia was a Philadelphia-based African-American organized crime syndicate founded around 1969 by Samuel Christian. It controlled drug trafficking, extortion, and other rackets in Philadelphia and the Delaware Valley during the 1970s and early 1980s. The group was known for its use of intimidation and violence, with over 40 murders attributed to it. It became largely defunct by 1984, though a successor group, the Junior Black Mafia, operated until 1992.

The Crips are a primarily African-American alliance of street gangs based in Southern California, founded in Los Angeles in 1969 by Raymond Washington and Stanley Williams. They are one of the largest and most violent street gang associations in the United States, with an estimated 30,000 to 35,000 members in 2008. Members are involved in murders, robberies, and drug dealing, and have a long-standing rivalry with the Bloods.

Brazilian organized crime syndicate founded in 1993 by eight prisoners at Taubaté Penitentiary. Based in São Paulo, it is Latin America's largest drug gang with 40,000 lifetime members and 60,000 contractors. Active throughout South America, West Africa, and Europe, it controls over 50% of Brazil's drug exports to Europe and has a partnership with the 'Ndrangheta. Known for a business model favoring quiet market expansion over violent turf wars.

The Latin Kings is a predominantly Hispanic street and prison gang founded in Chicago in 1954 by Puerto Ricans. It operates under two main factions: the King Motherland Chicago (KMC) and the Bloodline, with a total membership estimated between 22,000 and 42,500. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, assault, burglary, homicide, identity theft, and money laundering, with primary income from street-level narcotics distribution. It has a presence in 31 U.S. states and several other countries.

The Five Points Gang was a criminal street gang based in the Five Points of Lower Manhattan, New York City, during the late 19th and early 20th century. Founded by Paul Kelly (born Paolo Antonio Vaccarelli), it initially consisted primarily of Irish-Americans but later became predominantly Italian-American. The gang engaged in racketeering, extortion, illegal gambling, and other crimes. Many of its members, including Johnny Torrio, Al Capone, and Lucky Luciano, later became prominent criminals.

Japan's largest yakuza organization, founded in 1915 by Harukichi Yamaguchi in Kobe. It engages in extortion, gambling, prostitution, arms and drug trafficking, real estate kickbacks, and other criminal activities. Membership has declined sharply from its 1960s peak to 3,100 active members by 2025. The group operates across Japan and overseas, with headquarters in Kobe.

The Mexican Mafia, also known as La eMe, is a powerful criminal organization based in the United States, primarily composed of individuals of Mexican origin. Founded on November 15, 1957, by Luis "Huero Buff" Flores in a California youth prison, it is one of the oldest and most powerful prison gangs in the U.S. The organization controls illegal activities within prisons and on the streets, including drug trafficking, extortion, and murder. Despite its name, it has no origins in Mexico and operates almost exclusively in the United States.

The Sicilian Mafia, also known as Cosa Nostra, is an Italian criminal society originating in Sicily in the mid-19th century. It evolved from local protection and land control into a powerful criminal network involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, money laundering, and murder. The organization is structured into families or clans, each controlling a territory. It has influenced politics and finance and spawned diaspora groups like the American Mafia.

The Ghetto Boys is a street gang based in New Cross, Lewisham and Deptford, South London. Formed in the 1970s on the Woodpecker Estate, it is primarily composed of Black British members. The gang has been involved in drug trafficking, murder, assault, and robbery, and had a notable conflict with the Peckham Boys. By the mid-late 2000s, the gang declined and splintered, with offshoots like Shower and 814 emerging.

Russian state-funded private military company controlled by Yevgeny Prigozhin until 2023, then by his son Pavel. Emerged during the war in Donbas (2014-2015), played a major role in the Russian invasion of Ukraine, and supported regimes in Syria, Libya, Central African Republic, and Mali. Accused of war crimes and linked to far-right extremism. Led an armed rebellion against Russia in June 2023.

Chinese triad formed by former Red Guards in Chinese prison camps in the late 1960s, later relocating to Hong Kong in the 1970s. Known for armed robberies, drug trafficking, and extreme violence. Described as one of the largest and most sophisticated criminal conspiracies in Canada.

Los Zetas is a fractured Mexican criminal syndicate and designated terrorist organization, known for extreme violence including beheadings and torture. Originally formed in the late 1990s as the enforcement arm of the Gulf Cartel by deserters from the Mexican Army, they broke away in 2010 to become an independent cartel. They were once Mexico's largest cartel by territory but have fragmented since the mid-2010s, with many factions absorbed or eliminated. They are based in Nuevo Laredo, Tamaulipas, and engage in drug trafficking, extortion, kidnapping, and other illegal activities.

The Bandidos Motorcycle Club is a motorcycle gang classified as a criminal organization by law enforcement in multiple countries. In Denmark, the club has ten chapters and about 150 members involved in drug dealing, extortion, fraud, weapons trafficking, and violent crimes. The Danish Bandidos originated from the Morticians club, which rebranded as Undertakers and patched over by Bandidos in 1993. They were key participants in the Nordic Biker War (1994-1997) against the Hells Angels.

Aryan Nations is a North American neo-Nazi organization founded in the 1970s by Richard Girnt Butler, originally based in Hayden Lake, Idaho. It adheres to Christian Identity doctrine, a white supremacist interpretation of Christianity. The group was classified as a terrorist threat by the FBI in 2001 and described as the first nationwide terrorist network in the US and Canada. After Butler's death in 2004, the organization fragmented into several splinter groups.

Far-left Marxist-Leninist guerrilla group involved in the Colombian conflict, founded in 1964 from peasant self-defense groups. Funded by kidnap, ransom, extortion, and drug trafficking. Engaged in terrorism and unconventional warfare. Demobilized in 2017 after a peace agreement, though dissident factions remain active.

The Commisso 'ndrina is a powerful clan of the 'Ndrangheta based in Siderno, Calabria, Italy, with a branch in the Greater Toronto Area, Canada. Founded in the 1930s by Antonio Macrì, it is involved in international drug trafficking, racketeering, money laundering, and extortion. The clan emerged as a dominant force in Siderno after the First 'Ndrangheta war and has been active in conflicts with rival clans such as the Costa 'ndrina.

Mexican criminal organization dedicated to drug trafficking and arms trafficking, designated as a foreign terrorist organization by the United States and Argentina. Founded in 2007 as an armed wing under the name Los Mata Zetas, it became independent in 2010 and emerged publicly in September 2011. Led by Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes (El Mencho) from 2009 until his death in 2026, it is considered one of the most dangerous criminal groups in Mexico and a rival of the Sinaloa Cartel.

The Bloods are a predominantly African-American street gang founded in Los Angeles, California in 1972. They are known for their red attire and hand signs spelling 'Blood'. The gang consists of numerous subgroups called 'sets' and is engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, murder, robbery, and pimping. They have a long-standing rivalry with the Crips and have spread across the United States and Europe.

One of the earliest crime families in New York City, based in Italian Harlem. Founded by Giuseppe Morello in the 1890s, it dominated the Italian underworld by defeating the Brooklyn Camorra. The family engaged in racketeering, counterfeiting, and murder, and was the predecessor of the Genovese crime family. It was active until 1931.

The Gulf Cartel is a Mexican drug trafficking organization based in Matamoros, Tamaulipas, founded in the 1930s by Juan Nepomuceno Guerra. Initially smuggling alcohol during Prohibition, it later expanded into cocaine, methamphetamine, and marijuana under Juan García Ábrego. It is one of the oldest organized crime groups in Mexico and is designated as a terrorist organization by the United States. The cartel is known for its violence and international network.

Hamas is a Sunni Islamist Palestinian nationalist political organization with a military wing, the al-Qassam Brigades. Founded in 1987 by Ahmed Yassin during the First Intifada, it has governed the Gaza Strip since 2007. It advocates for Palestinian statehood and resistance against Israeli occupation, and has been involved in multiple conflicts with Israel.

Irish American criminal street gang active in Lower Manhattan from the 1830s to 1860s. They were founded by disgruntled members of the Roach Guards and were known for their rivalry with the Bowery Boys. The gang engaged in street fighting, assault, murder, and rioting, and supported Tammany Hall politicians. Their symbol was a dead rabbit on a pike.

Transnational criminal organization originating from Venezuela, formed around 2007 in Aragua state. Initially involved in extortion linked to a railway construction union, it consolidated power from 2013 at the Tocorón prison under Héctor 'Niño' Guerrero. Engages in drug trafficking, human trafficking, extortion, illegal mining, and money laundering across Latin America, the US, and Spain. Designated as a terrorist group by several countries.

Multi-generational gang based in Peckham, South London, founded in the 1990s. Known for popularising road rap music and involvement in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, robbery, and murder. The gang operates in Peckham, Walworth, and Camberwell, and is divided into subsets by age group. Active as of 2025.

The Corleonesi was an alliance of Mafia families within the Sicilian Mafia, formed in the 1970s. Led by Luciano Leggio, Salvatore Riina, Bernardo Provenzano, and others, it took over the Sicilian Mafia Commission and imposed a dictatorship over Cosa Nostra. The coalition waged the Second Mafia War from 1978–1983 and dominated until 2006.

The Aryan Brotherhood is a white supremacist prison gang and organized crime syndicate in the United States, founded in 1964 at San Quentin State Prison. With an estimated 20,000 members inside and outside prison, it is responsible for up to 25% of murders in the federal prison system despite comprising less than 3% of the inmate population. The gang engages in drug trafficking, extortion, inmate prostitution, and contract killings, and is known for its hierarchical leadership structure and distinctive tattoos.

The Clan dei Casalesi is one of the most powerful Camorra clans and criminal organizations in Italy. It was created by Antonio Bardellino and originated in the municipalities of San Cipriano d'Aversa and Casal di Principe in the second half of the 20th century. The clan is active in Spain, Switzerland, and Romania, involved in cocaine trafficking, prostitution, construction, and money laundering.

Neo-Nazi group formed in 1988 in Dallas, Texas, focused on producing and promoting white power rock music. It has chapters in the US, Canada, Europe, Australia, and New Zealand, and is described as the best-organized neo-Nazi skinhead group in the US. Members have been involved in violent attacks and hate crimes, though not centrally organized. The group maintains an active recruitment strategy and encourages military enlistment for a race war.

Sam Gor is an international crime syndicate based in Asia-Pacific, primarily involved in drug trafficking. It is made up of members from five triads and is headed by Chinese-Canadian Tse Chi Lop, who was arrested in 2021. The group controls an estimated 40-70% of the Asia-Pacific methamphetamine market and earns at least $8 billion per year. It is believed to manufacture meth in the Golden Triangle region of Myanmar.

International criminal gang originating in Los Angeles, California in the 1980s, initially formed to protect Salvadoran immigrants. It expanded through deportations to Central America and is active in the US, Canada, Mexico, and Central America. Engages in drug trafficking, murder, extortion, and other crimes. Designated as a terrorist organization by the US, Canada, and El Salvador.

Kurdish militant political organization and armed guerrilla group primarily based in mountainous Kurdish-majority regions of southeastern Turkey, northern Iraq, and northeastern Syria. Founded on 27 November 1978 in Ziyaret, Lice, by Abdullah Öcalan and others. Initially sought an independent Kurdish state, later shifted to seeking autonomy and cultural rights within Turkey. Designated as a terrorist organization by Turkey, the US, EU, and others. Declared a ceasefire in March 2025 and announced plans for dissolution.

The Taliban is an Afghan political and militant organization that emerged in 1994 during the Second Afghan Civil War. Founded by Mullah Omar, it ruled most of Afghanistan from 1996 to 2001, enforcing a strict interpretation of sharia law. Overthrown by a US-led invasion in 2001, it waged a 20-year insurgency before recapturing Kabul in 2021 and now controls all of Afghanistan. The group is designated as a terrorist organization by several countries and is widely condemned for human rights abuses.

The 14K is a triad group based in Hong Kong but active internationally. It is the second largest triad group in the world with around 20,000 members split into thirty subgroups. Formed in 1945 by Kuomintang Lieutenant-General Kot Siu-wong in Guangzhou, China, as an anti-Communist action group, it relocated to Hong Kong in 1949. The group is heavily involved in drug trafficking, illegal gambling, money laundering, and other criminal activities.

Italian American Mafia crime family based in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, founded around 1911 by Salvatore Sabella. It operates throughout the Greater Philadelphia metropolitan area and South Jersey, known for violence and internal conflicts. The family experienced a peaceful era under Angelo Bruno (1959-1980) before a series of violent successions and wars. Currently active, with membership estimated at 50 made members and 100 associates as of 2004.

The Clan Mazzarella is a Camorra criminal organization operating in several neighborhoods of Naples, Italy. Historically considered one of the most powerful Camorra clans, it was founded by the Zaza family in the 1940s and later came under the control of the Mazzarella brothers. Currently, it is divided into several branches, each with its own regent.

The Nuova Famiglia was an Italian Camorra confederation created in the late 1970s to oppose Raffaele Cutolo's Nuova Camorra Organizzata. It was headed by powerful Camorra bosses including Carmine Alfieri, the Nuvoletta brothers, Michele Zaza, Luigi Giuliano, and Antonio Bardellino. The organization was active primarily in Campania and had ties with the Sicilian Mafia. It engaged in various criminal activities such as drug trafficking, murder, and extortion until its dissolution in the 1990s.

Major Irish transnational organised crime syndicate founded by Christy Kinahan in the 1990s. Based in Dublin, it is one of the largest criminal groups globally, involved in drug trafficking, money laundering, and arms trafficking. Estimated wealth up to €1 billion. Engaged in a violent feud with the Hutch Gang since 2015.

The White Hand Gang was a collection of Irish American gangs on the New York City, Brooklyn, and Red Hook waterfronts from the early 1900s to 1925. They organized against the growing influence of Italian gangsters, choosing their name as a response to the Sicilian Black Hand gangs. Known for being virulently anti-Italian and particularly violent, the gang suffered from unstable leadership and internal killings, leading to its demise.

Sunni Islamist political and paramilitary group that fought in the Syrian civil war to overthrow Bashar al-Assad and establish an Islamic state. Formed in 2012, it was the official Syrian affiliate of Al-Qaeda from 2013 to 2016. In 2016 it broke ties with Al-Qaeda and renamed itself Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham, then merged into Hayat Tahrir Al-Sham in 2017.

The Genovese crime family is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the Five Families that dominate organized crime in New York City and New Jersey. Founded by Charles 'Lucky' Luciano in the 1930s, it is the oldest and largest of the Five Families. Known for its secrecy and white-collar rackets, it has been considered the most powerful Mafia family in the United States. The family is currently led by Liborio 'Barney' Bellomo.

International far-right extremist and neo-Nazi network formed in 2015 and based in the Southern United States. It expanded across the US and several other countries, described as one of the most violent neo-Nazi movements of the 21st century. Members have been responsible for murders, bombings, and planned terrorist attacks. Designated as a terrorist group by multiple governments including the UK and Canada.

Japanese organized crime syndicate dating back to the 17th century, known for strict codes of conduct, ritual practices like yubitsume, and full-body tattoos. It engages in extortion, drug trafficking, money laundering, and other illegal activities. Membership peaked in 1963 at 184,100 but declined to about 22,000 by 2022. The group remains active and sophisticated, with international operations.

Ahrar al-Sham was a Sunni Islamist rebel group that fought against the Ba'athist regime of Bashar al-Assad during the Syrian Civil War. Formed in 2011, it grew to become one of the largest opposition factions, with an estimated 20,000 fighters at its peak. The group aimed to establish an Islamic state under Sharia law and was supported by Turkey, Qatar, and Saudi Arabia. It dissolved in January 2025, merging into Syria's new Ministry of Defense.

The Shower Posse is a Jamaican gang founded around 1981 by Lester Lloyd Coke and Vivian Blake, based in Tivoli Gardens, Kingston. It is involved in drug and arms smuggling, with branches in North America and the United Kingdom. The gang has ties to the Jamaica Labour Party and has been linked to CIA support. It remains active in drug trafficking, firearms trafficking, and other criminal activities.

Regional branch of the Islamic State active in Central and South Asia, primarily Afghanistan and Pakistan. Founded on 26 January 2015 by former Tehreek-i-Taliban Pakistan members. Seeks to establish a caliphate in the historic Khorasan region. Responsible for numerous terror attacks, including the 2021 Kabul airport attack and the 2024 Crocus City Hall attack in Russia. Currently led by Shahab al-Muhajir.

The Hard Livings gang is a large street gang and organized crime group based in Manenberg, Cape Town, South Africa. Founded in 1971 by twins Rashied and Rashaad Staggie, it evolved from a street gang into a structured criminal organization involved in drug trafficking, weapon trafficking, prostitution, contract killing, extortion, illegal diamond smuggling, and other crimes. The gang was one of two 'super gangs' in Cape Town, rivaling The Americans, and has international connections including with the Sicilian Mafia. After Rashaad was killed by vigilante group PAGAD in 1996, and Rashied in 2019, the gang remains active.

Organised crime group based in Moss Side, South Manchester, England, founded in the late 1980s. Primarily involved in drug trafficking, weapon trafficking, robbery, and murder. Known for violent disputes with rival gangs such as the Doddington Gang and Moss Side Bloods. Still active as of the present.

The 'Ndrina Mancuso is a powerful 'Ndrangheta clan based in Limbadi and Nicotera, in the province of Vibo Valentia, Calabria. Considered the most influential clan in the province, it is involved in international cocaine trafficking, extortion, usury, public procurement fraud, and money laundering. The clan has alliances with other 'Ndrangheta families, Colombian cartels, and the FARC, and operates in northern Italy, central Italy, and abroad, notably in Togo. It has been described as the financially most powerful criminal clan in Europe.

The 'Ndrina De Stefano is a powerful 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Reggio Calabria, Italy, historically considered the most influential in the city. Founded by Domenico Tripodo, it later came under the leadership of Paolo De Stefano. The clan was involved in the first and second 'Ndrangheta wars and has expanded its operations to northern Italy and abroad, engaging in drug trafficking and other illegal activities.

Italian American Mafia crime family based in Chicago, Illinois, originating in the city's South Side in the early 1910s. Rose to power in the 1920s under Johnny Torrio and Al Capone during Prohibition. Involved in loansharking, illegal gambling, extortion, political corruption, and murder. Considered one of the most violent Mafia families in the U.S., with over 1,000 murders since the 1920s. Still active but in decline since the late 20th century.

orgs.n: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine
orgs.desc: Palestinian Marxist-Leninist organization founded in 1967 by George Habash. It is the second-largest faction in the Palestine Liberation Organization, opposing Fatah's moderate stance and advocating a one-state solution. The group pioneered aircraft hijackings in the late 1960s and early 1970s and has been designated a terrorist organization by several countries. Its military wing is the Abu Ali Mustafa Brigades.

An all-female London crime syndicate active from the late 19th century to the 1950s, specializing in shoplifting. Based in the Elephant and Castle area, they raided West End stores and sometimes posed as housemaids to rob homes. The gang was allied with the Elephant and Castle Mob and avoided police detection for decades.

The Americans is a large street gang and organized crime group based in the Cape Flats area of Cape Town, South Africa. Founded in 1983 by Neville Harold (Jackie Lonte), it is a corporate body for many smaller gangs and is aligned with the 26 and 27 prison number gangs. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, abalone poaching, and other criminal activities. It has an estimated 30,000 members and its headquarters is called The White House.

The United Wa State Army is the military wing of the United Wa State Party, the de facto ruling party of Wa State in Myanmar. Formed in 1989 after the collapse of the Communist Party of Burma, it is a well-equipped ethnic minority army of 20,000–30,000 soldiers led by Bao Youxiang. Despite being de facto independent, it officially recognizes Myanmar's sovereignty and has signed ceasefire and peace deals. It is the largest non-state armed group in Myanmar and leads the Federal Political Negotiation and Consultative Committee.

The South Brooklyn Boys (SBB) was a New York City street gang formed in the 1950s from several Italian-American neighborhood gangs in South Brooklyn. The gang engaged in assault, drug trafficking, extortion, bookmaking, and murder, and had loose affiliations with the Italian-American Mafia. It was active from the 1950s until the 2000s.

The Alvaro 'ndrina is a powerful 'Ndrangheta clan originating from Sinopoli, Calabria, with around 2,000 affiliates. It operates in various Italian provinces and has international branches in Australia. The clan rose to prominence after a 1945 feud and was involved in kidnappings and drug trafficking in the 1970s. It remains active, with recent operations revealing ties to other 'Ndrangheta clans.

The Medellín Cartel was a loose coalition of Colombian drug trafficking organizations based in Medellín, Colombia, that played a central role in the international cocaine trade during the late 1970s and 1980s. It was founded by Pablo Escobar, Carlos Lehder, Gonzalo Rodríguez Gacha, and the Ochoa brothers. The network was known for its violence, including terrorism and assassinations, and was designated a terrorist group by the United States. It was dismantled in the early 1990s following the death of Escobar.

Italian Camorra criminal organization founded in the late 1970s by Raffaele Cutolo in Campania. It was a structured, hierarchical organization that became the strongest Camorra clan by 1981, providing a living for at least 200,000 people in the Neapolitan area. It was hostile to the Sicilian Mafia but allied with 'Ndrangheta clans. The organization was considered extinct in the late 1980s after many members were killed or imprisoned.

The March 23 Movement (M23) is a Congolese rebel paramilitary group backed by Rwanda, based in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo. It was founded in 2012 by former members of the National Congress for the Defence of the People (CNDP) who mutinied against the government. M23 captured Goma in 2012 but withdrew after negotiations, and resumed operations in 2022. It is the principal member of the Congo River Alliance.

The 'Ndrina Grande Aracri is a 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cutro, Calabria, Italy. It operates extensively in northern Italy (Emilia-Romagna, Veneto, Lombardy) and abroad in Germany. The group is involved in drug trafficking, false invoicing, extortion targeting tourist villages, and construction fraud. It emerged in the 1980s under Nicolino Grande Aracri and has been engaged in feuds with rival families.

The 'Ndrina Morabito is a powerful 'Ndrangheta family from Africo, Calabria, Italy. It is involved in illicit activities in northern Italy and abroad, with branches in South America, Europe, and Africa. Its historical boss was Giuseppe Morabito, known as 'u tiradrittu', who was arrested in 2004. The clan has ties to other criminal organizations, including Mafia Capitale in Rome.

Organized crime group based in British Columbia, Canada, formed in the early 1990s by Indo-Canadians. Initially known as the Sunset Boys, they became independent from the Punjabi mafia after 1998. Engaged in drug trafficking, murder, and other crimes across Canada and in prisons. Known for red and black colors and the Independent Soldiers clothing brand.

A white supremacist prison gang active in Southern California, especially Cypress, with small factions in Chicago and other states. Originated in the mid-1970s, recognized by California prison authorities in 1999. Known for extreme violence and a three-tier hierarchy. Allies include the Aryan Brotherhood and Public Enemy No. 1; rivals include Bloods, Crips, and Black Guerrilla Family.

Canadian outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1965 in Oshawa, Ontario. It was the second largest outlaw motorcycle club in the world by 1970 with over 400 members. The club was involved in the first major outlaw biker conflict in Canadian history against the Popeyes from 1974 to 1976. It disbanded in 2000 when most chapters joined the Hells Angels, was briefly reactivated in 2017 and again in 2025.

Multi-ethnic street gang from Los Angeles, California, founded in the 1960s by Mexican-American and other immigrant youths. One of the largest street gangs in Los Angeles with 30,000–50,000 members across the US, Mexico, and Central America. Engages in drug trafficking, prostitution, extortion, kidnapping, robbery, assault, and murder. Designated as a terrorist group by the US, El Salvador, and Guatemala.

Deobandi jihadist militant organization primarily operating along the Afghanistan-Pakistan border. Founded by Baitullah Mehsud in 2007, it seeks to overthrow the Pakistani government and establish an Islamic state. Known for suicide bombings, sectarian violence, and attacks on security forces. Designated as a terrorist group by the UN and several countries.

Colombian narcoparamilitary and terrorist organization that emerged after the demobilization of the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC) between 2003 and 2006. It is considered the largest, most dangerous, and best-structured criminal group in Colombia, involved in drug trafficking, massacres, extortion, kidnapping, and other crimes. The group operates in hundreds of municipalities across Colombia and has an estimated 9,000 to 14,000 members. Its former leader Dairo Antonio Úsuga David (alias Otoniel) was captured in October 2021.

The Lucchese crime family is one of the Five Families of the American Mafia in New York City. Originating in the early 1920s, it was led by Tommy Gagliano and later Tommy Lucchese, becoming a powerful force in organized crime. The family controls rackets including gambling, loansharking, drug trafficking, and labor racketeering, primarily in New York and New Jersey. It is still active today.

The Mongols Motorcycle Club is an international outlaw motorcycle gang founded on December 5, 1969 in Montebello, California. The club is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, money laundering, and violent crimes. It has chapters in 28 countries and an estimated membership of 4,000–5,000. The club is designated as an outlaw motorcycle gang by law enforcement in multiple countries.

The Clan Cappello is a Mafia organization and family originating from the province of Catania, Sicily, named after boss Salvatore Cappello. It is considered, together with the Catania family of Nitto Santapaola, the most powerful Mafia clan in eastern Sicily. The clan emerged from the earlier Ferlito-Pillera group and rose to prominence in the 1980s under Salvatore Cappello.

ETA was an armed Basque nationalist and separatist organization active in Spain and France between 1959 and 2018. Founded in 1959 during Francoist Spain by Basque nationalist students, it evolved into a clandestine paramilitary group conducting assassinations, bombings, and kidnappings primarily in Spain. ETA killed over 800 people and was classified as a terrorist group by multiple countries. It announced a definitive end to armed activity in 2011 and formally dissolved in 2018.

The Mammoliti are a powerful 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Oppido Mamertina and Castellace, Calabria. They are involved in drug trafficking, extortion, agricultural activities, and have practiced kidnapping. They have bases in Germany, particularly in Baden-Württemberg and Bavaria. The Mammoliti-Rugolo are allied with the Piromalli, Varone, and Mazzaferro clans.

The Piromalli are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Gioia Tauro, Calabria. According to the DIA, they are the largest and most influential organized crime group in Western Europe, with over 400 allied families and thousands of affiliates. They have been active for over a century and are among the founders of the 'Ndrangheta. Their criminal activities include international drug and arms trafficking, agricultural fraud, and infiltration of the business sector.

The 'Ndrina Condello is a powerful 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Reggio Calabria, Villa San Giovanni, and Fiumara, Italy. It is involved in drug and arms trafficking, contract rigging, and extortion rackets. The clan was led by Pasquale Condello until his arrest in 2008, and later by Domenico Condello until his arrest in 2012. It participated in the Second 'Ndrangheta war against the De Stefano clan.

Predominantly Latino-American street gang based in San Fernando Valley, Glendale, with history dating back to the 1950s. They reside mainly in the Riverside Rancho neighborhood and are known for murder. Their main subset is Chicos Malos or Lake street. The gang is part of a larger Locos 13 alliance throughout Los Angeles County.

Organised street gang based in Tottenham, North London, originating on the Broadwater Farm estate in the 1970s. Involved in drug trafficking, weapon trafficking, armed robbery, kidnapping, and contract killing. Controlled much of the drug markets in North London during the 1990s. Splintered into various groups by the mid-2000s.

Colombian far-right paramilitary, drug trafficking, and counterinsurgent organization active from 1997 to 2006. Formed on April 18, 1997, as a coalition of paramilitary groups to combat leftist guerrillas such as FARC-EP and ELN. Responsible for numerous human rights abuses, including massacres, forced displacement, and assassinations. Designated as a terrorist organization by Colombia, the EU, and the US until 2014. Demobilized in 2006, with some members later forming successor criminal groups.

International outlaw motorcycle club founded in Montreal, Canada in 1986 by Salvatore Cazzetta and his brother Giovanni. It competed with the Hells Angels during the Quebec Biker War (1994-2002), resulting in over 160 deaths. The original club was patched over into the Bandidos in 2001, but a second version was revived in 2008. As of 2022, it has over 120 chapters worldwide.

The 'Ndrina Pesce is one of the most powerful 'ndrine of the 'Ndrangheta, based in Rosarno, Calabria. It has an army of affiliates organized in 30 'locali' and numerous 'ndrine, with interests extending from Reggio Calabria to Milan. The clan is involved in all trafficking in the Gioia Tauro area, including drugs, extortion, and control of agricultural markets. It has alliances with other major 'ndrine such as Piromalli, Mancuso, and Molè, and is active internationally with connections in Austria, Greece, Lebanon, Germany, and France.

Barriox13 is a highly active Sureños street gang based in South Los Angeles, founded in the early 1970s near El Segundo Boulevard and San Pedro Street. The gang is divided into West Side and East Side factions, with over 5,000 members loyal to the Mexican Mafia. It is involved in narcotics, homicide, drug and human trafficking, and has expanded into Southern California and Mexico.

Menace of Destruction (MOD) is a Hmong street gang founded in 1988 in Fresno, California. Originally called Masters of Destruction, it is one of the oldest active Asian street gangs in Northern California and the largest Hmong gang in the United States. The gang is involved in murders, shootings, drug trafficking (marijuana and methamphetamine), and weapon trafficking. It is active in California, the Midwestern United States, and other areas with large Hmong communities.

Jihadist militant and pirate group based in the Philippines, founded in 1991 by Abdurajak Abubakar Janjalani. It follows Wahhabi Sunni Islam and has been responsible for bombings, kidnappings, and assassinations, including the 2004 MV Superferry 14 bombing. The group aligned with the Islamic State in 2014. As of 2023, its membership had dwindled to about 20, and it was declared dismantled in 2024 but resurfaced in 2026.

One of the two largest criminal organizations in Brazil, founded in 1979 at the Cândido Mendes prison on Ilha Grande, Rio de Janeiro. It originated from the Falange Vermelha and was created by Rogério Lemgruber. The group is heavily involved in drug trafficking and operates in multiple Brazilian states and several South American countries. It is designated as a terrorist organization by Argentina, Paraguay, and the United States.

British street gang based in Brixton, South London, formed in the late 2000s. Known for drug trafficking, murder, theft, and money laundering. Considered one of the most dangerous gangs in South London by 2012, responsible for multiple murders and violent incidents. Membership declined in the early 2010s due to incarceration of leaders.

The 'Ndrina Ruga is a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Monasterace, Italy, dominant in the Stilaro Valley until the mid-1990s. Its activities include construction contracts, agricultural control, and kidnappings in the 1980s. The clan has been involved in drug trafficking and money laundering in Anzio and Nettuno, and has alliances with other 'ndrine. Many members were arrested in the 1994 Operation Stilaro, but some were later acquitted.

The Bellocco 'ndrina is a powerful organized crime clan from Rosarno, Calabria, part of the 'Ndrangheta. Active in drug and arms trafficking, extortion, and control of businesses, it operates mainly in Italy and has international connections. The clan played a key role in founding the Sacra Corona Unita in 1981. Its influence extends to Lombardy and other regions, with ties to other 'ndrine such as Pesce and Piromalli.

Colombian drug cartel based in Cali, founded by the Rodríguez Orejuela brothers and José Santacruz Londoño after breaking from Pablo Escobar in 1988. At its peak (1993-1995), it controlled over 80% of the world's cocaine market and was considered the most powerful criminal organization globally. The cartel operated through a decentralized cell structure and was dismantled by the mid-1990s.

Colombian left-wing guerrilla organization founded in 1967, based on Marxism-Leninism. Most of its members demobilized in 1991, but a dissident faction remains active in the Catatumbo region and the Colombia-Venezuela border. It is classified as an Organized Armed Group by the Colombian government.

American white supremacist and far-right hate group, founded in 1865 in Pulaski, Tennessee, by former Confederate soldiers. It has operated in three major iterations, using violence and intimidation to target African Americans, Jews, Catholics, and immigrants. The Klan is considered one of the earliest terrorist groups in the United States, with a history of lynching, political violence, and racial terror. Modern iterations are smaller but continue to promote white nationalism and anti-immigrant nativism.

The 'Ndrina Arena is a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta criminal organization based in Isola Capo Rizzuto, Calabria, Italy. It has influence in the Catanzaro area through alliances with local clans. The clan has been involved in drug and arms trafficking, counterfeiting, extortion, and murder, with operations extending to Brazil and Germany. Key figures include Nicola Arena and Carmine Arena.

The Lo Presti 'ndrina is a Mafia clan of the 'Ndrangheta, originating from Marina di Gioiosa Jonica in Calabria and established in Bardonecchia, Piedmont, in the late 1950s. Initially part of the Mazzaferro family, it became an independent 'ndrina. It is known for infiltration of public administration, leading to the dissolution of the Bardonecchia city council in 1995. The clan has been involved in drug and arms trafficking, extortion, and usury.

The Musitano crime family is a 'Ndrangheta organized crime family based in Hamilton, Ontario, Canada, with branches in Italy. Founded by Angelo Musitano in the 1940s, it engaged in racketeering, drug trafficking, and murder. The family was involved in a power struggle with rival Hamilton families, leading to the killings of Johnny Papalia and Carmen Barillaro in 1997. Key members Angelo and Pat Musitano were killed in 2017 and 2020, respectively.

The Bonanno crime family is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the Five Families that dominate organized crime in New York City. Founded by Salvatore Maranzano in the 1890s, it was later led by Joseph Bonanno. The family has been involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, and labor union corruption, with operations extending into New Jersey, Florida, California, Arizona, and Montreal. Historically one of the smallest Five Families, it was known for its brutality and internal conflicts such as the Banana War.

The Elephant and Castle Mob was an independent street gang active in London's underworld during the interwar years. They were rivals of Charles Sabini and the Birmingham Boys, and were eventually forced out by Sabini with Sicilian mafiosi. The gang achieved dominance on race courses and in London's West End from the 1910s to the 1930s, led by the McDonald brothers. They disappeared by the end of World War II.

Organized crime group operating in Sweden, Norway, and Denmark, founded in the 1970s by Željko "Arkan" Ražnatović. Based in Stockholm and Copenhagen, it engages in cigarette smuggling, drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, and other crimes. The group gained media attention in the 1990s and is currently led by Milan Ševo.

The Banda della Magliana was a mafia-type criminal organization based in Rome, Italy, active from the late 1970s until 1999. It unified the fragmented local underworld under a single structure, engaging in kidnappings, gambling, drug trafficking, and robberies. The group had connections with other Italian criminal organizations, Masonic lodges, far-right terrorists, and intelligence services. It was involved in several high-profile Italian historical events, including the Moro case and the disappearance of Emanuela Orlandi.

The Gallico are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) based in Palmi, Italy, active in extortion, drug trafficking, and infiltration of public contracts. They are the strongest family in the municipalities of Palmi, Barritteri, Melicuccà, and Seminara, and also operate in Rome. The clan has been involved in a long-running feud with the Parrello-Condello and Bruzzise clans, which ended in 1990. In 1994, Teresa Concetta Managò became the first female pentito of the 'Ndrangheta in a trial against the Gallico clan.

Haitian-American street gang founded in 1990 in Miami, Florida, initially as a vigilante self-defense group. Engages in drug trafficking, robbery, murder, prostitution, and racketeering. Expanded across Florida and other states, with territory reaching Virginia by 2006. A faction called ZMF was involved in the 2018 murder of XXXTentacion.

Sudanese paramilitary force originating from the Janjaweed militias used in the Darfur conflict. Formed in August 2013 under Muhammad Dagalo (Hemedti). Fights the Sudanese Armed Forces in a civil war since 2023, establishing a parallel government in 2025. Accused of war crimes, genocide, and human rights abuses.

The Catania Mafia family is a crime family of the Sicilian Cosa Nostra that dominates organized crime in Catania and its province. Founded around 1925 by Antonino Saitta, it evolved into a powerful and violent faction under leaders like Giuseppe Calderone and Benedetto Santapaola. The organization is involved in extortion, drug trafficking, and infiltration of legitimate businesses, and remains active today under the Santapaola-Ercolano clan.

The Comanchero Motorcycle Club is an outlaw motorcycle gang based in Australia, with chapters in Southeast Asia and New Zealand. Founded in 1968 by William George 'Jock' Ross in Sydney, the club is known for its violent history, including the 1984 Milperra massacre. The Comancheros are involved in drug trafficking and have been engaged in ongoing conflicts with rival clubs such as the Hells Angels and Bandidos.

Italian American Mafia crime family based in Buffalo, New York, founded around 1910 by Angelo Palmeri. It operates throughout Western New York, Erie, Pennsylvania, and Hamilton, Ontario, Canada. The family was led for decades by Stefano Magaddino, a member of The Commission, and is currently headed by Joseph A. Todaro Jr. It is involved in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, drug trafficking, and other illegal activities.

The PDC was a street gang based in Brixton, London, England, primarily active in the 1990s. They were involved in drug trafficking, robberies, and violence, and were the largest gang in the SW9 postcode. The gang later reformed as a music collective and label, while a faction broke off to form the 'Muslim Boys'.

The Amato-Pagano clan, also known as the Scissionisti di Secondigliano, is a Camorra clan from the Secondigliano district of Naples, founded in 2004 by Raffaele Amato and Cesare Pagano. It split from the Di Lauro clan and engaged in a violent feud known as the Scampia feud, resulting in over 100 murders. The clan is heavily involved in international drug trafficking, particularly cocaine, with a strong presence in Spain. Currently, it is one of the most important Camorra organizations.

Pakistani Islamist jihadist militant organization founded in 1985–1986 by Hafiz Saeed and others with funding from Osama bin Laden. Its primary objective is to merge Kashmir with Pakistan and seeks destruction of India, Hinduism, and Judaism through jihad. Designated as a terrorist group by the UN and many countries, it has carried out major attacks in India including the 2008 Mumbai attacks. It is supported by Pakistan's ISI and operates primarily in Kashmir.

Sacra Corona Unita is a Mafia-type criminal organization from the Apulia region in Southern Italy, founded in the early 1980s by Pino Rogoli. It is active in areas such as Brindisi, Lecce, and Taranto, and engages in cigarette smuggling, arms trafficking, drug trafficking, human trafficking, money laundering, extortion, and political corruption. The group was formed to counter the power of other mafia organizations in prisons and Apulian territory. After arrests and defections in the early 2000s, it no longer exists as a single viable organization.

The 'Ndrina Gioffrè is a powerful 'Ndrangheta clan based in Seminara, Calabria, Italy. It is involved in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, cigarette smuggling, prostitution, illegal waste disposal, infiltration of local government, and money laundering. The clan emerged victorious in the Faida di Seminara in the 1970s and later experienced internal conflicts in the 2000s. It remains active.

Outlaw motorcycle club formed by Lou Dobkin in 1959 in Prince George's County, Maryland. Known for territorial conflicts with the Hells Angels and other clubs. Active across the United States and Puerto Rico with over 100 chapters. Involved in traditional organized crime, including ties to La Cosa Nostra.

The West End Gang is a powerful criminal organization based in Montreal, Quebec, active since the early 1900s. It is recognized as part of the Irish mafia, with close ties to the Winter Hill Gang in Boston and alliances with the Rizzuto and Musitano crime families. The gang engages in theft, robbery, kidnapping, fraud, arms trafficking, and drug dealing, notably controlling cocaine and hashish distribution in the 1970s. It was part of the Consorzio, a drug cartel with Italian-Canadian families and Hells Angels. Current membership is estimated at 100-150, and the gang remains active under boss Gerald Matticks.

African American prison gang, street gang, and political organization founded in 1966 by George Jackson, George 'Big Jake' Lewis, and W.L. Nolen at San Quentin State Prison in California. It is a Marxist-Leninist revolutionary group promoting black power and prisoner dignity, involved in drug trafficking, burglary, and homicide. The BGF remains active with estimated 100-300 members and thousands of associates.

Sun Yee On is one of the leading triads in Hong Kong and China, founded in 1919 by Heung Chin. It has over 25,000 members worldwide and is active in multiple countries including the UK, US, France, and Belgium. The triad is involved in racketeering, counterfeiting, extortion, drug trafficking, money laundering, murder, and illegal gambling. It remains active as of the present day.

The 'Ndrina Gallace is a powerful 'Ndrangheta clan originating from Guardavalle, Italy. Over the last 30 years, it has expanded its operations across Italy, including Tuscany, Lazio, Lombardy, and Rome. The clan is heavily involved in international drug trafficking, fraud, usury, and extortion. It is allied with other 'Ndrangheta clans such as Farao-Marincola and Leuzzi.

The Houthis are a Zaydi revivalist and Shia Islamist political and military organization that emerged from Yemen in the 1990s. Founded by Hussein al-Houthi, the group opposes the Yemeni government and is backed by Iran. It has been a central player in Yemen's civil war, launching attacks on Saudi Arabia, Israel, and Red Sea shipping. The movement is designated as a terrorist organization by several countries.

Far-left political party and guerrilla group in Peru, founded in 1969 by Abimael Guzmán. It launched a 'people's war' in 1980 aiming to overthrow the government and establish a New Democracy through guerrilla warfare. The group is designated as a terrorist organization by Peru, the US, EU, Japan, and Canada. Since the capture of its leaders in the 1990s and 2012, its activity has declined, with remaining factions operating in the VRAEM region.

The Clan dei Casamonica is a Romani mafia organization based in Rome, Italy, originating from Abruzzo and Molise. Founded in the late 1930s by Luciano Casamonica and Adelaide Spada, it evolved from semi-nomadic horse traders into a structured criminal group. The clan controls various illegal activities in Rome and Lazio, with ties to the Banda della Magliana. It remains active today.

The D'Agostino are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Reggio Calabria, Italy, with branches in Rosarno, Laureana di Borrello, Sant'Ilario dello Ionio, and Canolo. They are historically powerful, acting as a link between Reggio and Locri clans and representing the 'Ndrangheta within Cosa Nostra in the 1970s. They have been involved in international drug trafficking and feuds, such as the Faida di Laureana and Faida di Sant'Ilario. Many members are currently imprisoned, but the organization remains active.

The Sin City Deciples Motorcycle Club is a mixed-race one-percenter outlaw motorcycle club founded in Gary, Indiana in 1967. It is one of the oldest and most well-known black outlaw motorcycle clubs in the United States, with chapters across the U.S. and international presence. The club is involved in illicit drug sales and is regarded as a criminal organization by law enforcement.

Puerto Rican criminal organization founded in the 1980s by Quitoni Martinez in Aguada, Puerto Rico. It operates as a mafia-style group with six crime families, involved in drug trafficking, racketeering, extortion, and money laundering. The organization has connections with Colombian cartels and other international crime groups, and maintains a presence in the United States, Mexico, Spain, and Panama.

Mexican drug cartel founded in 1996 by the five Beltrán Leyva brothers, originally part of the Sinaloa Cartel. It trafficked cocaine, heroin, and marijuana, and engaged in human smuggling, money laundering, extortion, kidnapping, and murder. The cartel infiltrated Mexican government agencies and Interpol. It was declared disbanded in 2011 after the arrest of its last major lieutenant, though remnants continued fighting until 2017.

The Clan Licciardi is a powerful Camorra syndicate based in the Secondigliano district of Naples, Italy. Founded in the mid-1980s by Gennaro Licciardi, it transformed the area into a strategic hub for drug storage and trafficking. After Gennaro's death, leadership passed to his siblings, notably Maria Licciardi. The clan is part of the Alleanza di Secondigliano cartel and has been involved in internal conflicts and large-scale drug trafficking.

The Detroit Partnership is an Italian American Mafia crime family based in Detroit, Michigan, founded around 1908 by Pietro Mirabile. It operates primarily in the Greater Detroit area, with additional territory in Ohio, Ontario, and Las Vegas. The family has been involved in racketeering, gambling, loan-sharking, labor racketeering, narcotics trafficking, and other illegal activities. Despite RICO prosecutions in the 1990s, the organization remains active in the 21st century.

The United Nations (UN) is a criminal gang that originated in Abbotsford, British Columbia, Canada, founded in 1997 by Clayton Roueche and a group of high-school friends. The gang is involved in arms trafficking, drug trafficking, extortion, illegal immigration, money laundering, murder, and other crimes. It operates primarily in Canada and the United States, with alliances including the Triads of Vancouver and the Sinaloa Cartel.

The Dojin-kai is a yakuza organization headquartered in Kurume, Fukuoka, Japan, founded in 1971 by Isoji Koga. It is a designated yakuza syndicate with approximately 310 members, known for its militant nature and large-scale drug trafficking, particularly methamphetamine. The group has been involved in violent conflicts with other yakuza groups, including the Yamaguchi-gumi and its own splinter group, Kyushu Seido-kai.

The Almighty Vice Lord Nation is the second-largest street and prison gang in Chicago, Illinois, founded in 1957 by African American youths in the North Lawndale neighborhood. It is a founding member of the People Nation alliance and engages in drug sales, robbery, extortion, and murder. The gang attempted to soften its image in the 1960s as the Conservative Vice Lords but continued criminal activities. It remains active with an estimated 30,000–35,000 members.

orgs.n: Gang de la Brise de Mer
orgs.desc: Corsican criminal organization founded in the 1970s, based in Northern Corsica. Controls racketeering, slot machines, money laundering, night clubs, gambling, and casinos in Corsica, southern France, Paris, Italy, Africa, and Latin America. Known for violent robberies and conflicts with rival gangs. Estimated capital between 120 and 150 million euros.

The Grey Wolves are a Turkish far-right ultranationalist and neo-fascist political movement and the youth wing of the Nationalist Movement Party (MHP). Founded by Colonel Alparslan Türkeş in the late 1960s, they engaged in urban guerrilla warfare during the late 1970s political violence in Turkey and are implicated in numerous massacres and assassinations. The organization has been banned in Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan, and France, and is active in Turkey, Northern Cyprus, Western Europe, and Central Asia.

Mexican drug cartel based in Ciudad Juárez, Chihuahua, founded around the 1970s by Pablo Acosta Villarreal, Rafael Aguilar Guajardo, Amado Carrillo Fuentes, and Vicente Carrillo Fuentes. It controls drug trafficking routes into the United States and is known for extreme violence, including decapitations. Its armed wing is La Línea. The cartel declined after the death of Amado Carrillo Fuentes in 1997 but remains active.

American criminal street gang based in Los Angeles, California, composed mainly of Mexican Americans. Named after the Florence area of Los Angeles County and controlled by the Mexican Mafia. Involved in drug smuggling, murder, assault, robbery, and other crimes. Active since the 1930s with an estimated 2,000-3,000+ members.

The 'Ndrina Iannazzo is a 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria, based in Lamezia Terme. It is allied with the Giampà and Cannizzaro-Daponte clans. The group has been involved in a long-running feud with the Torcasio-Gualtieri-Cerra clan, resulting in dozens of murders. It has also infiltrated public contracts, including the modernization of the A3 motorway, and has been linked to political corruption. In 2022, assets worth €800 million were seized from its associates.

The Secondigliano Alliance is a strategic alliance of Camorra clans based in Naples, Italy, founded in the late 1980s by Gennaro Licciardi, Edoardo Contini, and Francesco Mallardo. It controls drug trafficking and extortion rackets in Naples and its hinterland, with branches in the Netherlands and Spain. Considered one of the two most powerful Camorra organizations, it has been led by Maria Licciardi and later Vincenzo Licciardi. Despite police crackdowns, it remains active.

Primarily ethnically Turkish criminal gang active in Germany, the Netherlands, and Denmark. Formed in 1985 in Heidenheim an der Brenz by immigrants from Turkey, Italy, and former Yugoslavia. Engages in arms trafficking, drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, arson, prostitution, burglary, and vandalism. Claims to be a brotherhood rather than an outlaw motorcycle club or criminal organization.

The Vallelunga, also known as the Viperari, are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Serra San Bruno, Calabria, Italy. Active since the 1960s, they are considered the hegemonic clan in the mountainous territory spanning the provinces of Catanzaro, Vibo Valentia, and Reggio Calabria. They have been involved in numerous feuds for territorial control, including the Faida dei boschi in the late 1980s, and are regarded as one of the most powerful and bloodiest clans in Calabria.

The Latin Counts is an Indigenous Latino American street gang based in Chicago, Illinois, with a significant branch in Detroit, Michigan. Founded in 1955 by Tejano youth in the Pilsen neighborhood, it is a member of the People Nation alliance. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, racketeering, and other criminal activities.

Southeast Asian Islamist militant group based in Indonesia, founded on 1 January 1993 by Abu Bakar Bashir and Abdullah Sungkar. Dedicated to establishing an Islamic state in Southeast Asia, it perpetrated the 2002 Bali bombings and other attacks. Designated as a terrorist group by the UN and multiple countries. Disbanded in 2024.

The Clan Cava is a Camorra organization based in Quindici, province of Avellino, Italy. It has been involved in a bloody feud with the rival Graziano clan for over thirty years. The clan operates in the Vallo di Lauro, Baianese, Nolano, and other areas of Campania, engaging in extortion, contract rigging, usury, and drug trafficking. Despite state crackdowns, it remains one of the most influential clans in the province of Avellino.

Outlaw motorcycle club founded in San Bernardino, California in 1961 by John J. 'Cadillac Jack' Baltas. Has chapters across the United States, with a significant presence in New England. Known for its red-and-black devil insignia and considered a second-tier outlaw motorcycle gang by law enforcement.

The 'Ndrina Barbaro is a powerful 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta, originating from Platì, Calabria, Italy. Founded in the late 19th century, it is one of the oldest still-active 'ndrine. The family has multiple branches and is heavily involved in drug trafficking and arms possession. It has expanded its operations across northern Italy and into Australia, particularly in Melbourne and Griffith.

Camorra clan operating in the eastern suburbs of Naples, specifically Ponticelli and Cercola. Founded by Antonio De Luca Bossa, known as 'o sicco, born in 1971. The clan was hostile to the Fusco-Ponticelli and Sarno clans, engaging in a bloody war for territorial control. After the arrest of its founder and the killing of his successor Giuseppe Mignano, the clan was eliminated by the Sarno clan.

The Fabbrocino clan is a Camorra mafia-type criminal organization originating from the area between Ottaviano, San Gennaro Vesuviano, and San Giuseppe Vesuviano in Italy. It was founded by Mario Fabbrocino after breaking away from Michele Zaza's group. The clan controls a vast territory in the Vesuvian area and is involved in diverse criminal activities including extortion, vote-buying, and money laundering through legitimate businesses. Mario Fabbrocino was sentenced to life imprisonment for the murder of Roberto Cutolo.

Irish republican paramilitary force that sought to end British rule in Northern Ireland and create an independent socialist republic. It emerged in December 1969 from a split in the IRA and was the most active republican group during the Troubles. The IRA used guerrilla tactics and bombings, killing over 1,700 people. It declared a ceasefire in 1997 and formally ended its campaign in 2005.

The 'Ndrina Barreca is a 'ndrina (criminal family) of the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria, based in the southern area of Reggio Calabria, active since the 1980s. It operates in the neighborhoods of Pellaro, Bocale, and Lazzaro. Originally part of the Ambrogio clan, it split off during the Pellaro feud in the late 1980s. The clan has been involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and violent feuds, and several members have become pentiti (collaborators with justice).

Italian American Mafia crime family based in Cleveland, Ohio, formed during the 1900s. Under boss John T. Scalish (1945-1976), it exerted influence over the Teamsters and skimmed Las Vegas casino revenue. Membership peaked at 60 made men in the 1950s. After a gang war in the late 1970s and subsequent convictions, the family weakened but remains active in illegal gambling and loansharking.

The Fruit Town Piru is a set of the Piru gang alliance, part of the larger Bloods alliance, formed in the 1970s in Compton, California. Named after fruit-named streets in their neighborhood, the gang is primarily African-American and known for its rivalry with the Mob Piru Bloods. It has been involved in violent feuds and has affiliations with rappers such as Chris Brown and Soulja Boy.

Chinese American criminal organization and street gang founded in San Francisco, California in 1964. Involved in narcotics, racketeering, gambling, and other illegal activities. Known for the 1977 Golden Dragon Massacre and conflicts with rival gangs. Active primarily in the San Francisco Bay Area and Greater Los Angeles.

Ecuadorian criminal and terrorist organization specializing in drug trafficking and contract killing. Formed in 2020 as a splinter group from Los Choneros after the death of Jorge Luis Zambrano. Operates primarily in Latacunga, Riobamba, Cuenca, Machala, and Pastaza province, with over 8,000 members. Involved in prison riots and political violence, including the assassination of presidential candidate Fernando Villavicencio.

The Avenues is a Mexican/Mexican American criminal street gang based in Los Angeles County, California. Originally a social club founded by the Flores brothers in the 1940s, it evolved into a violent criminal organization involved in drug trafficking, murder, and hate crimes. The gang is under the control of the Mexican Mafia in prison and has an estimated 800 members. It remains active as of the present day.

Ah Kong was a Singaporean organized crime syndicate that controlled the European heroin trade from the 1970s to the 1990s. Based in Amsterdam and Bangkok, it was one of the world's largest drug syndicates, sourcing heroin from the Golden Triangle. The gang was formed by Singaporean Chinese who fled to the Netherlands after a gang clash. It declined after its boss was assassinated in 1997 and the last official boss died in 2010.

Mexican drug cartel and organized crime syndicate based in Michoacán, founded in the 1980s by Carlos Rosales Mendoza. Known for producing large amounts of methamphetamine. Originally allied with the Gulf Cartel and Los Zetas, it split off in 2006. After the death of leader Nazario Moreno González, several factions split to form the Knights Templar Cartel and La Nueva Familia Michoacana.

Informal Chechnya-based detachments of Russian state security forces, effectively serving as Ramzan Kadyrov's private army. Founded in the late 1990s, they have been accused of widespread human rights abuses including torture and extrajudicial killings. They have participated in conflicts such as the Syrian Civil War and the Russian invasion of Ukraine.

The Pecoraro-Renna clan is a Camorra clan originating from Battipaglia, Italy. Founded by Giovanni Pecoraro after his release from prison, it fought against the Cutolo and De Feo clans in the Sele Plain. After Giovanni's murder in 1988, his brothers Alfonso and Francesco took over, leading a bloody feud. The clan is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, usury, prostitution, and tax evasion, with operations in the Sele Plain and surrounding areas.

The Winter Hill Gang was a loose confederation of American organized crime figures based in the Boston area, primarily active from 1961 to 2000. Founded by James 'Buddy' McLean and Howard 'Howie' Winter, it was predominantly Irish-American and involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, gambling, and weapons smuggling. The gang was the second most powerful criminal organization in New England after the Patriarca crime family. It was taken over by James 'Whitey' Bulger in 1979 and eventually dissolved.

The South Side Compton Crips are a set of the Crips gang alliance based in Compton, California, formed in the 1970s. They are primarily African-American and known for their involvement in the murder of Tupac Shakur in 1996. The gang operated numerous drug houses during the crack epidemic and has ongoing rivalries with various Piru and Crips sets.

The Clan Giuliano is a Camorra organization based in central Naples, founded by Pio Vittorio Giuliano in the 1950s. It was one of the most powerful criminal groups in Campania from the 1970s to the 1990s, involved in cigarette smuggling, drug trafficking, and counterfeiting. The clan operated in neighborhoods such as Forcella, San Gaetano, Maddalena, Piazza Garibaldi, and Piazza Mercato. Several family members became pentiti (collaborators with justice), leading to its decline.

The Facchineri are a historical 'ndrina based in Cittanova, Calabria, operating since the late 19th century. They engage in extortion, management of commercial activities, and drug trafficking, with branches in Puglia, Umbria, and Lazio. They were involved in heroin trafficking and kidnappings in the past. The group has been involved in multiple feuds, notably with the Raso-Albanese-Gullace clans, and has connections to the Banda della Magliana in Rome.

The Almighty Insane Popes Nation is a European American street gang based in Chicago, Illinois, formed in the late 1950s. Originally a Greek greaser gang, it adopted the name Popes, standing for 'Protecting Our People Eliminating Spics' (later changed to 'scum'). The gang expanded to include Polish, German, Irish, and Scandinavian members, and later invited Latino and black members. It split into North Side and South Side factions with different alliances.

Sunni Islamist militant group based in Somalia, formed in the mid-2000s as a youth militia within the Islamic Courts Union. It controls territory in south and southwestern Somalia, is allied with al-Qaeda, and has carried out numerous high-fatality attacks in Somalia and neighboring countries. Despite territorial losses after 2011, it remains active with an estimated 7,000–18,000 fighters.

The 'Ndrina Palamara is a 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Africo, Calabria, Italy. It is allied with the Bruzzaniti and Morabito clans. The group has been involved in drug trafficking, particularly cocaine from South America via Senegal, and in the rigging of public contracts in the tourism and hydroelectric sectors. Members have been arrested in multiple operations, including 'Naos' in 2008.

The Red Scorpions is a Canadian gang based in British Columbia, formed in 2000 by Quang Vinh Thang Le, Konaam Shirzad, and others. They are involved in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, and other criminal activities, operating primarily in the Lower Mainland of Vancouver. The gang is known for its rivalry with the United Nations gang and has been linked to high-profile gang violence. Members are identified by 'RS' tattoos.

Originally founded as an enforcement wing and debt collection service for Pablo Escobar's Medellín Cartel, Oficina de Envigado evolved into a major drug cartel and criminal organization based in Medellín and Envigado, Colombia. It controls extortion, gambling, and money laundering in the Valle de Aburrá and acts as a mediator and debt collector in drug trafficking disputes. By 2019, its activities shifted from direct drug trafficking to providing services to lower-level traffickers and mafia groups. It maintains connections with Colombian paramilitaries and guerrillas.

The Albanese are a powerful 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cittanova and Laureana di Borrello, Calabria. They have branches in Brescia, Tuscany, Lazio, and Liguria. Their activities include drug trafficking, grape market control, and past kidnappings. They are allied with the Raso-Gullace clan and have been involved in feuds with other clans.

International outlaw motorcycle club founded in Woodbridge, Ontario, Canada in 1979 by Frank Lenti and Gennaro Raso. Operates chapters in Canada, Italy, the United States, and other countries. Known for its elaborate patch and involvement in conflicts with other biker gangs. Estimated to have 1,000 active members globally.

A powerful 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta from San Luca, Calabria, Italy. Historical rivals of the Pelle 'ndrina, they are known for the San Luca feud and the 2007 Duisburg massacre. Involved in drug trafficking and other criminal activities.

Russian crime syndicate founded in the late 1980s in the Solntsevo District of Moscow by Sergei Mikhailov and Viktor Averin. It controls transport hubs and engages in racketeering, drug trafficking, money laundering, and other crimes. The group has expanded internationally and maintains alliances with various criminal organizations.

Islamist militant organization active in North Africa, originating as the Salafist Group for Preaching and Combat (GSPC) in Algeria. It rebranded as AQIM in 2007 and is aligned with al-Qaeda. Engages in insurgency in the Maghreb and Sahel, funding itself largely through kidnapping for ransom. In 2017, its Sahara branch merged with other groups to form Jama'at Nasr al-Islam wal Muslimin.

Los Choneros is an organized crime syndicate from Ecuador, involved in drug trafficking, extortion, robbery, and other crimes. Originating in Manabí Province in the 1990s, it functions as the armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel in Ecuador. The group has significant influence over security officials and politicians, and its leader José Adolfo Macías Villamar escaped prison in 2024, triggering an armed conflict. As of 2025, the group remains active with an estimated 12,000-20,000 members.

American Marxist militant organization active from 1969 to 1977, originally a faction of Students for a Democratic Society. It sought to overthrow the U.S. government through a bombing campaign targeting government buildings and banks, with no fatalities from its bombings. The group was designated a domestic terrorist organization by the FBI and dissolved after the Vietnam War peace accord.

Primarily Cambodian-American street gang based in Long Beach, California, founded in 1981 by Cambodian youth refugees for protection. Engages in drug trafficking, murder, robbery, and weapons trafficking. Has an estimated 5,000-10,000 members and associates as of 2009. Active in the United States and Canada.

The Nutty Blocc Compton Crips are a set of the Crips gang alliance based in Compton, California. They are one of the larger Crip sets in Compton and have been involved in gang wars, notably with the ATF alliance in 1995. Several influential rappers have been associated with the gang.

Black Cobra is one of the largest immigrant street gangs in Denmark, founded in 2000 in Roskilde. With approximately 100 members, it operates in major Danish cities and has expanded into Sweden and Norway. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, and murder, and is known for its distinctive black and white shirts with a cobra emblem. It controls a youth gang called the Black Scorpions.

Italian Canadian crime family based in Montreal, Quebec, founded in the 1970s by Nicolo Rizzuto. It overtook the Cotroni crime family to become the preeminent Mafia organization in Montreal. The family is involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, illegal gambling, and murder. It is considered by the FBI as a faction of the Bonanno crime family, but recognized as independent by Canadian authorities.

The Chosen Few Motorcycle Club is the first mixed-race 1%er outlaw motorcycle club, founded around 1959 in Los Angeles, California by Lionel Ricks and others. It has chapters across the United States and internationally in the Philippines. The club is known for its outlaw status and diverse membership.

Historically one of the most influential Mafia clans in Sicily, based in Ciaculli and Croceverde Giardini near Palermo. Founded in the late 19th century, the extended family played a key role in the Sicilian Cosa Nostra, with members serving as secretaries of the Sicilian Mafia Commission. Involved in citrus growing, cigarette smuggling, heroin trafficking, and money laundering. Declined in the 2000s but remains active with strong influence.

The Clan Ascione is a Camorra organization originating and operating in the Ercolano area of Italy. It emerged in the 1980s, defeating the Esposito clan in a local feud. The clan is involved in racketeering and other criminal activities. Its current status is unclear from the text.

The Tongan Crip Gang is a Tongan American street gang and a subset of the Crips alliance, founded in Inglewood, California. It is active in California, Utah, Alaska, Washington, and parts of Auckland, New Zealand. The gang engages in a wide range of criminal activities including drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, and murder. It was involved in a 2007 federal indictment for a conspiracy to murder members of MS-13.

Mexican criminal organization founded in 2009 in Tepito, Mexico City, under the tutelage of Édgar Valdez Villarreal. It engages in drug trafficking, kidnapping, extortion, murder, money laundering, and other crimes. The group operates primarily in Greater Mexico City and several Mexican states, and is known for using minors in criminal activities. It remains active as of 2021.

The Kodo-kai is a yakuza criminal organization based in Nagoya, Japan, founded in 1984 by Shinobu Tsukasa. It is a secondary organization of the Sixth Yamaguchi-gumi, the largest yakuza syndicate in Japan, with an estimated membership of 4,000. The Kodo-kai is known for its defiant attitude toward police and its involvement in banking, real estate, securities, and show business. It is considered by the National Police Agency to be more like a mafia-type or terrorist organization.

The Eastman Gang was a predominantly Jewish-American street gang that dominated parts of the New York City underworld from the late 1890s to the early 1910s. Founded by Monk Eastman, the gang operated in Manhattan's Lower East Side, engaging in armed robbery, theft, illegal gambling, extortion, prostitution, and opium peddling. It succeeded the Whyos as a prominent non-Irish gang and had strong political connections through Tammany Hall. The gang dissolved in the early 1910s.

A notorious larrikin gang that dominated The Rocks area of Sydney from the 1870s to the 1890s. Engaged in theft, murder, assault, and robbery, often using female members to lure victims. Leaders were determined through bare-knuckle boxing matches. Known for a distinctive dress style to appear unarmed while concealing razor blades.

International outlaw motorcycle club formed in Glasgow, Scotland in 1963 by Allan Morrison and Billy Gordon. It is the oldest outlaw biker club in Europe and one of the largest in the UK, with chapters in Scotland, England, Belgium, Spain, and Germany. The club has been linked to organized crime and designated a criminal motorcycle gang by Belgian federal police.

The Gypsy Joker Motorcycle Club is a one-percenter motorcycle club founded in San Bernardino, California on April 1, 1956. It expanded internationally, gaining notoriety in Australia, the United States, Germany, and Norway. The club is known for its violent criminal activities, including drug trafficking, homicide, and arson. In Australia, it is considered one of the most violent bikie gangs and was involved in the 2001 car bomb murder of former detective Don Hancock.

The Mob Piru is a set of the Piru gang alliance and a criminal organization, part of the larger Bloods alliance. Based in East Compton, California, it is primarily African American and involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and arms trafficking. Suge Knight, co-founder of Death Row Records, is an affiliate. The set split from Lueders Park Piru at some point.

The Bahala Na Gang is a Filipino street gang founded in the early 1940s in Sampaloc, Manila, by Divino Talastas. It originated as a protective group against rival gangs and later expanded into criminal activities including drug and weapons trafficking. The gang spread to the United States and remains active, with alliances including Nuestra Familia.

The 116th Street Crew is a faction of the Genovese crime family based in East Harlem, Manhattan. Founded in the 1890s by the Morello crime family, it was originally known as the 116th Street Mob. The crew engaged in racketeering, murder, extortion, illegal gambling, loansharking, and bookmaking. It absorbed former members of the East Harlem Purple Gang in the late 1970s and early 1980s.

Mexican drug trafficking organization based in Tijuana, Baja California, founded in 1989 by the Arellano Félix brothers after the fragmentation of the Guadalajara Cartel. It was one of the largest and most violent cartels in Mexico during the 1980s and 1990s, but has been weakened by arrests and deaths of its leaders. Since 2014, it has allied with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel, effectively merging into the CTNG.

Canadian outlaw biker gang and support club for the Hells Angels, founded in Montreal on March 26, 1992 by Maurice 'Mom' Boucher. Active until 2001, it served as a recruitment tool and enforcement arm for the Hells Angels during the Quebec Biker War, carrying out intimidation, assaults, and assassinations to control the illegal drug trade.

The Bonavota are a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Sant'Onofrio and Stefanaconi, in the province of Vibo Valentia, Calabria. They have members in Piedmont (Turin, Carmagnola, Moncalieri) and Rome, and are allied with the Anello, Lo Bianco, and Mancuso clans. Their activities include extortion, usury, drug trafficking, and money laundering. They were involved in a bloody feud against the Petrolo-Bartolotta clans in the 1980s-1990s.
